'''Jayden Okunbor''' (born 2 March 1997) is an Australian professional [[rugby league]] footballer who plays as a {{rlp|wg}}er and {{rlp|sr}} forward for the [[Bradford Bulls]] in the [[Super League]].

==Background==
Okunbor was born in [[Sydney]], New South Wales, Australia, and is of [[Zimbabwe]]an and [[Nigerian]] descent on his father's side.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.nrl.com/news/2019/03/27/nigeria-to-belmore-and-back-tears-for-okunbors-stirring-bulldogs-debut/|title=Belmore to Nigeria and back: Tears for Okunbor's stirring NRL debut|date=27 March 2019|website=National Rugby League}}</ref><ref>{{cite web|date=4 September 2019|title=Meet Elliot Wallis – The explosive Hull KR winger called up by new Nigerian national team|url=https://www.mirror.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/meet-elliot-wallis-explosive-hull-19437350|publisher=Mirror|accessdate=1 November 2019}}</ref>
 
Okunbor played his junior football for the [[Milperra Colts]] and attended [[Picnic Point High School]] before being signed by the [[Canterbury-Bankstown Bulldogs]].<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.smh.com.au/sport/nrl/local-junior-okunbor-to-get-first-start-after-pay-swings-axe-at-dogs-20190326-p517s0.html|title=Local junior Okunbor to get first start after Pay swings axe at Dogs|first=Christian|last=Nicolussi|date=26 March 2019|website=The Sydney Morning Herald}}</ref>

==Playing career==
===Early career===
Okunbor played in the Bulldogs [[NRL Under-20s|Under-20s]] team in 2015–2017.
[[File:Jayden Okunbor 2016.png|thumb|Okunbor after playing a trial match for the Bulldogs against the [[Melbourne Storm]] at [[Belmore Oval]] in February 2016]] 
Okunbor spent 2018 playing in the Bulldogs 2018 [[New South Wales Cup|NSW Cup]] premiership winning team. On 23 September 2018, Okunbor played on the wing in the Bulldogs NSW Cup Grand Final against the [[Newtown Jets]] in the 18–12 victory at [[Leichhardt Oval]].<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.foxsports.com.au/nrl/nrl-premiership/teams/bulldogs/canterbury-bulldogs-beat-newtown-jets-to-win-2018-intrust-super-premiership-grand-final/news-story/5194d899167222fcbc817a6dfe96cf09|title=NSW Cup grand final 2018: Bulldogs v Newtown Jets|date=23 September 2018}}</ref> In the following week, on 30 September 2018, Okunbor played on the Wing in the State Championship Final against [[Queensland Cup]] winners the [[Redcliffe Dolphins]], scoring a try in the 42–18 win.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.nrl.com/news/2018/09/30/bulldogs-defeat-redcliffe-dolphins-to-win-intrust-super-championship/|title=Bulldogs down Dolphins to win Intrust Super Championship|date=30 September 2018|website=National Rugby League}}</ref>

===2019===
Okunbor made his [[National Rugby League]] (NRL) debut in round 3 of the [[2019 NRL season]] for the [[Canterbury-Bankstown Bulldogs]] against the [[Wests Tigers]], playing on the wing and having a solid match registering 214 metres in Canterbury's 22–8 win at [[Campbelltown Stadium]].<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.theaustralian.com.au/subscribe/news/1/?sourceCode=TAWEB_WRE170_a&dest=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.theaustralian.com.au%2Fsport%2Fnrl%2Fjayden-okunbors-perseverance-gets-its-reward-at-the-bulldogs%2Fnews-story%2F972911f6c50fcc36114ed40354fff468&memtype=anonymous&mode=premium&nk=cbf6ebbeff90a9fbd21ceab404e81c83-1592821570|title=Subscribe to The Australian | Newspaper home delivery, website, iPad, iPhone & Android apps|website=www.theaustralian.com.au}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.smh.com.au/sport/nrl/pay-celebrates-new-deal-with-upset-win-over-tigers-20190331-p519cd.html|title=Bulldogs mark Pay's new deal with upset win over Tigers|first=Adam|last=Pengilly|date=31 March 2019|website=The Sydney Morning Herald}}</ref> In the next match in Round 4 against the [[Melbourne Storm]], Okunbor had a powerhouse performance, scoring his first NRL career try, and ran for a whopping 290 metres even busting through 2 tackles from Storm captain [[Cameron Smith (rugby league, born 1983)|Cameron Smith]] during Canterbury's unlucky 18–16 loss at [[AAMI Park]].<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.dailytelegraph.com.au/subscribe/news/1/?sourceCode=DTWEB_WRE170_a&dest=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.dailytelegraph.com.au%2Fsport%2Fnrl%2Fteams%2Fbulldogs%2Fbrave-bulldogs-put-up-a-fight-but-fall-to-storm-in-melbourne-1816%2Fnews-story%2F598ee072d5ca78ec3225609e5fb2dd41&memtype=anonymous&mode=premium&nk=7d31f7375c2ea1c20c5b13e94a929319-1592821570|title=Dailytelegraph.com.au | Subscribe to The Daily Telegraph for exclusive stories|website=www.dailytelegraph.com.au}}</ref> On 29 April 2019, Okunbor was rewarded with a 2-year extension with Canterbury to the end of the 2021 season.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.nrl.com/news/2019/04/10/bulldogs-move-to-tie-up-rampaging-rookie-okunbor/|title=Bulldogs move to tie up record breaking rookie Okunbor|date=9 April 2019|website=National Rugby League}}</ref>

On 21 May, Okunbor was ruled out for 6 weeks after suffering a toe ligament injury in Canterbury's Round 10 victory over the Gold Coast where Okunbor had scored 2 tries.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.sportingnews.com/au/league/news/jayden-okunbor-nrl-injury-canterbury-bulldogs/efl4h2radpr91bbhrw7wa03td/|title=Jayden Okunbor sidelined for four to six weeks with toe injury|website=Sporting News}}</ref>

Okunbor made a total of 10 appearances for Canterbury in the [[2019 NRL season]] as the club finished 12th on the table.  At one stage, Canterbury-Bankstown found themselves sitting last on the table and in real danger of finishing with the wooden spoon.  However, for the third straight season, Canterbury achieved four upset victories in a row over [[Penrith Panthers|Penrith]], the [[Wests Tigers]], [[South Sydney Rabbitohs|South Sydney]] and [[Parramatta Eels|Parramatta]] who were all competing for a place in the finals series and were higher on the table.  Pay was credited with the late season revival as the side focused heavily on defence.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.brisbanetimes.com.au/sport/spoons-of-wood-high-hopes-and-chances-lost-the-final-round-storylines-20190905-p52o87.html/|title=Spoons of wood, high hopes and chances lost... the final round storylines|website=Brisbane Times}}</ref><ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.nrl.com/news/2019/09/01/the-dean-pay-way-unashamedly-old-school/|title=The Dean Pay way: 'Unashamedly' old school|website=NRL}}</ref>

===2020===
After being reinstated to the Canterbury club, Okunbor played his first game of the [[2020 NRL season]] in round 18 against Manly-Warringah where Canterbury lost 32–20.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.foxsports.com.au/nrl/nrl-premiership/teams/sea-eagles/nrl-2020-canterburybankstown-bulldogs-manlywarringah-sea-eagles-morgan-harper-comes-back-to-haunt-old-club/news-story/d59dca0e527a815a1d6bd6e98f33da0d/|title=Remember me? Morgan Harper couldn't get a run at the Dogs. He just came back to haunt them|website=www.foxsports.com.au}}</ref>

=== 2021 ===
Okunbor missed the first half of the [[2021 NRL season]] due to an injury to his [[Anterior cruciate ligament injury|Anterior Cruciate Ligament]], suffered in Canterbury's final game of the 2020 NRL season against the [[Penrith Panthers]].<ref>{{Cite web|title=Jayden Okunbor injury: Emerging Bulldogs star suffers suspected ACL rupture|url=https://www.sportingnews.com/au/league/news/nrl-2020-jayden-okunbor-ruptures-acl/1notq5kknztyg1e4una0ueihsz|access-date=2021-07-21|website=www.sportingnews.com}}</ref> He played his first game in Round 18 and scored a try in Canterbury's 32–24 loss to the [[South Sydney Rabbitohs]].<ref>{{Cite web|date=2021-07-18|title=Lewis binned for bizarre brain snap as Bunnies bounce Bulldogs|url=https://www.nrl.com/news/2021/07/18/lewis-sin-binned-for-bizarre-brain-snap-on-walk-off-to-half-time/|access-date=2021-07-21|website=National Rugby League}}</ref>

On 20 July, the Canterbury club announced that Okunbor had re-signed a contract for a further two-years, keeping him at the club until the end of 2023.<ref>{{Cite web|date=2021-07-20|title=Local junior Jayden Okunbor commits to the Bulldogs for another two seasons|url=https://www.bulldogs.com.au/news/2021/07/20/local-junior-jayden-okunbor-commits/|access-date=2021-07-21|website=Canterbury-Bankstown Bulldogs}}</ref>

Okunbor made a total of seven appearances for Canterbury and scored five tries in the [[2021 NRL season]] as the club finished last and claimed the [[wooden spoon (award)|Wooden Spoon]].<ref>{{cite web|url=https://wwos.nine.com.au/nrl/canterbury-bulldogs-set-to-sign-max-king-and-braidon-burns/a9fa54c9-a224-4cc9-ae15-8a5dd1e74275/|title=Canterbury Bulldogs set to sign Rabbitohs star Braidon Burns, Storm prop Max King for 2022 NRL season|website=wwos.nine.com.au}}</ref>

===2022===
Okunbor was limited to only six matches for Canterbury in the [[2022 NRL season]] as the club finished 12th on the table. Okunbor played for Canterbury in their 2022 [[NSW Cup]] Grand Final loss to [[Penrith Panthers|Penrith]].<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.sportingnews.com/au/rugby-league/news/nrl-2022-canterbury-bulldogs-season-review/wapmyddrbadukioadiybr1fs/|title=NRL 2022: Canterbury Bankstown Bulldogs season review|website=www.sportingnews.com}}</ref>

===2023===
In round 5 of the [[2023 NRL season]], Okunbor was sent to the sin bin for a hip drop tackle during Canterbury's 15-14 golden point extra-time victory over North Queensland.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.abc.net.au/news/2023-04-03/matt-burton-field-goal-golden-point-canterbury-bulldogs/102177894/|title=Canterbury's Matt Burton has everything an NRL team would want in their star but he can still have so much more|website=www.abc.net.au}}</ref>
In round 12, Okunbor scored the winning try for Canterbury with less than two minutes remaining as they defeated the Gold Coast 20–18.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.abc.net.au/news/2023-05-21/nrl-bulldogs-titans-raiders-sea-eagles/102359698/|title=Tom Trbojevic firms for NSW State of Origin selection with hat-trick in Sea Eagles' 42-14 NRL win over Raiders|website=www.abc.net.au}}</ref>
On 25 August, Okunbor signed a contract to join English side [[Hull F.C.]] ahead of the 2024 Super League season.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.hulldailymail.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/hull-fc-sign-nrl-forward-8701821/|title=Hull FC sign NRL forward Jayden Okunbor as overseas recruitment nears completion|website=www.hulldailymail.co.uk}}</ref>

===2024 & 2025===
Okunbor made his club debut for Hull F.C. against arch-rivals [[Hull Kingston Rovers]] in round 1 of the 2024 Super League season. Hull F.C. would go on to lose the match 22-0.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/68304214|title=Hull FC 0-22 Hull KR - Rovers win season opener as hosts have two sent off|website=www.bbc.co.uk}}</ref>

On 12 May 2024 it was reported that he had signed for [[Bradford Bulls|Bradford]] in the [[RFL Championship]] on short-term loan<ref>{{cite web|website=Total RL|date=12 May 2024|author=Callum Walker|title=Hull FC recruit Jayden Okunbor makes short-term loan move|url=https://www.totalrl.com/hull-fc-recruit-jayden-okunbor-makes-short-term-loan-move/}}</ref>

On 13 August 2024, it was confirmed that the move to Bradford was permanent. He later signed a new two year deal that coincided with Bradford's return to the Super League.<ref>{{cite web|website=Total RL|date=13 Aug 2024|accessdate=6 Nov 2024|author=Callum Walker|title=New Bradford Bulls signing Jayden Okunbor admits he was “close to just giving it away” following Hull FC spell|url=https://www.totalrl.com/new-bradford-bulls-signing-jayden-okunbor-admits-he-was-close-to-just-giving-it-away-following-hull-fc-spell/}}</ref>

===2026===
On 25 February, it was announced that Okunbor would miss around three months with a knee injury.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.alloutrugbyleague.co.uk/news/super-league-treatment-room-hull-1699803/|title=Super League treatment room as Hull FC, Hull KR, and St Helens pick up fresh injuries|website=www.alloutrugbyleague.co.uk}}</ref>
